data.title: Comfort Systems USA Is Maintained at Buy by UBS
UK Supreme Court to rule on motor finance commissions on August 1
data.description:
The United Kingdom's Supreme Court will give its long-awaited ruling on motor finance commissions, which will inform a potentially multi-billion pound consumer redress scheme, on August 1, the court announced on Friday.

British lender Close Brothers and South Africa's FirstRand are seeking to overturn a landmark Court of Appeal judgment, which said brokers must have customers' fully informed consent to receive a commission from lenders.

Britain's Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) is considering a redress scheme which could leave brokers paying out tens of billions of pounds – and has previously said it will confirm whether to do so within six weeks of the Supreme Court's ruling.

data.title: Grifols SA expected to post earnings of 20 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Grifols SA , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on July 29 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Sant Cugat Del Valles Barcelona-based company is expected to report a 2.2% increase in revenue to €1.858 billion from €1.82 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Grifols SA is for earnings of 20 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Grifols SA is $11.50, about 13.6% above its last closing price of $9.94

data.title: Pinnacle shares tumble as $8.6 billion deal for Synovus raises fears of stricter oversight
data.description:
Shares of U.S. regional banks Pinnacle Financial Partners and Synovus Financial fell on Friday as investors balked at the possibility of stricter regulatory requirements due to their merger, which would significantly increase their combined assets.

The combined entity, with more than $115 billion in assets, would cross the threshold to be classified as a "large financial institution," subject to much stricter regulations.

Analysts said that surpassing the $100 billion threshold often negatively affects bank profitability.

"The broader balance sheet impact may not be fully captured in the company's current projections and that it could represent an incremental headwind to profitability," Jefferies analysts wrote in a note.

"It necessitates growth in high-quality liquid asset securities, which typically yield less than loans."

Shares of Synovus, which has more assets than Pinnacle, tumbled 12.7% in morning trading, while Pinnacle, which has a larger market capitalization, also declined, according to LSEG data.

Expectations of lower interest rates and positive economic data have improved executive sentiment, a marked reversal from early April when U.S. tariff policy put dealmaking on hold.

Analysts remain cautious about the timing of Pinnacle’s decision. “Pinnacle’s organic growth model was strong enough not to require a deal that could disrupt the existing strategy,” J.P. Morgan analysts said.

Some analysts said that crossing the asset mark could provide a buffer for a stronger balance sheet and enhance operational efficiency.

"The combination includes two growth-oriented companies with a long history in large, growing Southeast markets giving a solid start out of the gate," analysts at Raymond James said in a note.

U.S. bank M&A activity is expected to accelerate in the second half of the year, supported by a more accommodating regulatory environment and a new merger review process.

data.title: FTSE 100 Snaps 6-Day Winning Streak
data.description:
The FTSE 100 fell on Friday, snapping a six-day winning streak, after weaker-than-expected UK retail data.

Retail sales rose 0.9% in June, missing forecasts, while May’s sharp drop was revised down to -2.8%, the worst since December 2023.

Consumer sentiment also dipped, with GfK's index falling as households brace for possible autumn tax hikes.

Rightmove fell more than 2% despite beating H1 profit estimates, as it warned that growth in H2 would slow.

JD Sports closed slightly lower but well off session lows, hit by concerns over US tariffs after Puma slashed its earnings forecast. However, it noted improving property market trends, with June listings and demand up 9% and 6% year-on-year.

On the upside, NatWest rose almost 2.5% after raising its full-year income outlook to over £16 billion and announcing a £750 million share buyback for H2 2025.

Despite Friday’s dip, the FTSE 100 booked its fifth straight weekly gain, hovering near record highs.

data.title: US FDA says J&J's Ethicon issues correction related to surgical stapler
data.description:
The U.S. drug regulator said on Friday Ethicon Endo-Surgery, a unit of Johnson & Johnson , issued a correction for certain lots of a part related to its surgical stapler and classified the action as "most serious".

The issue is related to the device 'Endopath Echelon Vascular White Reload for Advanced Placement Tip' which tends to inadvertently lockout during surgical procedures, the Food and Drug Administration said on its website.

"There has been one reported death and one injury related to this issue," the agency added.

The part in question is a single-use cartridge that surgeons load into a stapler to cut and close blood vessels or tissue during an operation. This helps control bleeding and close wounds quickly.

However, the FDA said the device may sometimes appear to work but fail to cut or staple tissue during surgery, and could lead to life-threatening bleeding, surgical delays, or death.

Ethicon notified its affected customers through a correction letter in April, but the company's action is not a full product recall, the agency said.

The FDA said Ethicon has advised healthcare facilities to review affected batches and ensure operating room personnel are familiar with the device's instructions for use and lockout mitigation protocols.

Johnson & Johnson did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Genworth Financial shares rally after legal ruling
data.description:
** Shares in Genworth Financial jumping ~7% on Friday, on track for biggest one-day pct gain since June 18 after a legal ruling in the financial services co's favor

** Genworth said it expects to receive ~$750 mln in proceeds after French insurer AXA AXAF.PA won a London court ruling on Friday worth nearly 675 million pounds ($908 million) against Santander SAN.MC over the mis-selling of payment protection insurance more than two decades ago

** AXA inherited liabilities in a 2015 acquisition of two Genworth units, but AXA will only be entitled to a minority of the award as Genworth had already reimbursed AXA for a substantial portion of mis-selling losses paid out

** Genworth said it plans to deploy any recoveries in line with priorities it listed as investing in growth through CareScout, returning cash to shareholders through buybacks, opportunistically paying down debt.

** GNW shares were last at $7.86, up 12.2% YTD

data.title: M/I HOMES, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
M/I Homes, Inc., a leading homebuilder in the United States, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped its recent results. This summary delves into the financial highlights and business activities that have defined M/I Homes' performance in the latest quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $1,162.6 million, increased 5% from the second quarter of 2024, driven by a 6% increase in the number of homes delivered.
  • Gross Margin: 24.7%, decreased 320 basis points from the second quarter of 2024, primarily due to higher lot costs and increased incentives offered.
  • Operating Income: $155.7 million, decreased from $186.8 million in the second quarter of 2024, impacted by a decline in gross margin and increased selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income: $121.2 million, decreased 17% from $146.7 million in the second quarter of 2024, reflecting the impact of reduced operating income.
  • Diluted EPS: $4.42 per share, down from $5.12 per share in the second quarter of 2024, consistent with the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in three main segments: Northern homebuilding, Southern homebuilding, and financial services. The Northern homebuilding segment includes operations in Chicago, Cincinnati, Columbus, Indianapolis, Minneapolis/St. Paul, and Detroit. The Southern homebuilding segment covers Ft. Myers/Naples, Orlando, Sarasota, Tampa, Austin, Dallas/Fort Worth, Houston, San Antonio, Charlotte, Raleigh, and Nashville. Financial services primarily support the homebuilding segments by providing mortgage and title services.
  • Geographical Performance: The Northern region saw a 3% increase in revenue due to a rise in the average sales price and the number of homes delivered. However, new contracts decreased by 13%, and backlog decreased by 21% due to weakened market demand. The Southern region experienced a 5% increase in revenue driven by an 8% increase in homes delivered, though operating income decreased due to higher lot costs and incentives. New contracts in the Southern region decreased by 4%, and backlog decreased by 28%.
  • Sales Units: During the first half of 2025, the company delivered 4,324 homes, a 1% decrease from the previous year. The Northern region delivered 967 homes in the second quarter, while the Southern region delivered 1,381 homes. The company started construction on 4,698 homes and ended the quarter with 5,062 homes under construction.
  • New Community Openings: The company opened 50 new communities and closed 36 communities in the first half of 2025, ending the second quarter with 234 active communities, compared to 211 at the end of the previous year's second quarter.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that the softer market conditions will persist throughout the remainder of 2025. It plans to manage overhead, land investments, and development expenditures prudently while maintaining pricing discipline and offering targeted incentives to support demand. The company expects to grow its average community count by approximately 5% by the end of 2025.
  • Operational Strategy: The company plans to promote sales through interest rate buydowns and other incentives, manage land spend and inventory levels, control construction cycle times, open new communities, and maintain a strong balance sheet and liquidity levels. Emphasis will also be placed on customer service, product quality, design, and premier locations.

SEC Filing: M/I HOMES, INC. [ MHO ] - 10-Q - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: Intel's Harsh Reality Nullifies Tariff Boost — WSJ
data.description:
By Dan Gallagher

In a way, Intel just reported its best quarter in years. But given the harsh realities the chip maker is facing, that's no longer enough.

Strong shipments of personal computers by manufacturers looking to get ahead of tariffs helped drive Intel's second-quarter revenue to nearly $12.9 billion, which was about 8% higher than Wall Street's estimates. That was the largest such beat in more than four years, according to data from FactSet.

But Intel's shares still slid 9% Friday morning. That is because Intel also used its earnings report to send a rather candid message about the slow-going turnaround of its manufacturing operations. New chief executive Lip-Bu Tan said the company's next chip manufacturing process, called 18A, will be used mostly for the company's own products. The one after-called 14A-will require at least one major external customer to be economically attractive enough to warrant further investment by the company, Tan said.

Several Wall Street analysts saw that as a line in the sand that might eventually signal the company's exit from chip manufacturing altogether. But that could clash with the U.S. government's efforts to build up domestic chip making capacity-a plan that requires the participation of the largest U.S.-based chip maker.

In a note, Chris Danely of Citigroup said Intel exiting chip manufacturing would be a good move, given its longstanding problems in catching up with industry leader TSMC. "However, we question the feasibility of this given geopolitical risk," Danely wrote.

In the current political environment, Intel may not be the full master of its own destiny.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Capital restructuring and share transfer, current status and measures
data.description:
a.i.s. AG

by Dr. Johan Bendien

Business news for the stock market

a.i.s. AG: Capital restructuring and share transfer, current status and measures

Berlin (pta000/25.07.2025/17:35 UTC+2)

As of the March date mentioned before to the financial market, it was not possible to determine a sufficient majority of the shares to be transferred to the investor's securities account. We have therefore extended further the deadlines as stated in the announcement dated 15 April 2025.

We also had to adjust this date, but have now completed the transfer of the shares on 4 July 2025. This means that 50% of the shares have been allocated to the deposit account of the rightful owner. In general, the approach chosen by the management appears to be feasible, even if the target of 73.5% has not been achieved. We are therefore dependent on a capital measure that will enable us to lay the foundations for restoring share trading. Corresponding discussions are planned for this month with the intention of allowing trading planned by August.

a.i.s. AG is in advanced talks with investors who will secure the restoration of the share capital. The structural change resolved at the Annual General Meeting on 23 January 2024 and the planned capital increase should also be viewed in this context. We will report on this next month once the lawful distribution of shares and stock exchange trading have been secured.

25 July 2025

(end)

data.title: ICE Canola Showing Independent Strength
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were stronger in the middle of Friday trading despite mostly negative sentiment in comparable oils.

European rapeseed was mostly higher, while Chicago soyoil and Malaysian palm oil were lower. Crude oil was down on Chevron's potential resumption of operations in Venezuela.

The Canadian Prairies will see scattered rainfall and thunderstorms today and this weekend. Central and southern Alberta are expected to receive the heaviest precipitation.

The Canadian dollar was down one-third of a U.S. cent compared to Thursday's close.

About 16,200 canola contracts have traded at 10:10 CDT.

 
Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton: 
 
Canola     Price        Change 
 Nov       698.80       up 3.10 
 Jan       708.30       up 3.20 
 Mar       713.10       up 1.50 
 May       718.20       up 2.30

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Healthpeak Properties Shares Decline on Lower 2Q Profit, Guidance Revision
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Healthpeak Properties shares declined 6.5% following a sharp drop in profit and revised guidance for 2025.

Shares were trading around $17.64, on pace for its largest percentage decrease since March 10, 2023. The stock is among the worst performers in the S&P 500 during Friday's trading session.

The company, whose focus includes managing healthcare real estate across the U.S., logged earnings of $31.7 million, or 5 cents a share, down from $146 million, or 21 cents a share, in the prior-year period.

Healthpeak also revised its earnings per-share forecast for the year downward to between 25 cents and 31 cents from prior guidance in the range of 30 cents to 36 cents.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: 'Pretty Good Chance' of EU Trade Deal, But Not With Canada, Trump Says — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Brian Swint

The impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs is showing up in companies' second-quarter earnings reports. It's not as bad as many would have thought three months ago.

To be sure, the taxes on imports are still set to raise companies' costs and weigh on profits — evidence so far suggests that it's corporate America that's paying for the tariffs most, rather than foreign firms or U.S. consumers. That may change in the future if companies raise prices to pass on the costs. And with the Aug. 1 deadline for getting trade deals with partners approaching next week, broad tariff rates look likely to be higher than they were in the second quarter.

But investors have been pleasantly surprised at the relatively small effects of the tariffs so far.

Deckers Outdoor, the maker of Ugg and Hoka shoes, declined to give guidance for more than the current quarter because of trade uncertainty. But the guidance it did give wasn't bad — it sees earnings per share rising to $1.50 to $1.55 from $0.93 last quarter. The stock was up 13% in premarket trading Friday.

Boston Beer, the maker of Sam Adams, said that the tariffs would cost the company between $15 million to $20 million for the full year. That's a lot, but less than the $30 million it had reckoned with before. Its shares jumped 7.5% before the market opened.

Chip maker Intel's results were ahead of analyst estimates and the company said that the numbers were helped by lower tariffs than feared. The company still has problems — it's laying off about 15% of its workforce, and its shares were slipping 7.8% Friday morning — but tariffs aren't one of the bigger ones.

A week from today, tariff rates are set to revert to higher ones for countries that haven't yet secured a deal. After getting to an agreement with Japan earlier this week, a pact with the European Union, the biggest U.S. trading partner, and India are expected to be announced soon.

Write to Brian Swint at brian.swint@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
